name: Socket.IO handshake reachable by anonymous callers
description: GETs the Engine.IO polling handshake (/socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=polling) with no credentials. A Socket.IO server with no authentication on the handshake answers any client with an Engine.IO open packet (a leading '0' followed by a JSON handshake carrying the session id and the available upgrade transports). That session id is the ticket to opening the live connection and emitting/subscribing to events, so an open handshake is the pre-auth entry point to the real-time surface. The probe proves the handshake is anonymous; whether individual event handlers are themselves authorized is a separate check, but an open handshake is the necessary first step and a strong misconfiguration signal on its own.

vulnerability.remediation: Authenticate the Socket.IO handshake, not just individual events. Validate a token in the handshake auth payload (client `io({ auth: { token } })`, server `io.use((socket, next) => …)` middleware) and reject unauthenticated connections before the open packet is sent. Verify with `curl -k 'https://host/socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=polling'` — a gated server returns 401/403, not a `0{"sid":…}` open packet. If the namespace is intentionally public, keep every event handler individually authorized.
